lesson: LOW MIX with a full hopper and alarms creeping up over weeks points to a scaled feed line, not a dead sensor. Gradual onset is the tell — descale it.

tests.error-log: LOW MIX alarms increasing in frequency over the past month.
tests.inspect-beater: Mix feed line is furred shut with white scale. Like a kettle, but load-bearing.

symptoms: LOW MIX alarm — staff refilled the hopper twice today already.
Flow into the barrel is a dribble.

tests.error-log: LOW MIX events roughly doubling week over week for a month.
tests.inspect-beater: Feed line furred with white scale, bore down to a drinking straw.

symptoms: Machine starves during the lunch rush but recovers when quiet.
LOW MIX alarm only at peak hours.

tests.error-log: Every LOW MIX alarm this month timestamps inside the lunch rush.
tests.inspect-beater: Scale narrowing the mix line — enough flow at idle, nowhere near enough at full draw.

flavour: Hard water, soft serve, hardest part was not laughing.
authenticityNote: Scale builds wherever water flows — mix lines fur up exactly like espresso machine boilers (the GDD §2.2 coffee-tech crossover). The tell versus a dead level sensor is the gradual onset: scale chokes the line over weeks, a sensor dies overnight.
